place: Fort Ann village, New York, New York

facts.text: Fort Ann’s Old Stone House Library, built in 1825, now houses the town’s historical society and a collection of early maps.
The village was renamed Fort Ann in 1808 after Queen Anne, following the 1711 reconstruction of Queen’s Fort by English forces.
In 1823 the Champlain Canal opened, linking Fort Ann to the rest of New York and turning the village into a bustling shipping hub.
The Delaware and Hudson Railway later built a depot in Fort Ann, making the village a key stop on the regional rail line.
Fort Ann’s early history includes several colonial forts, from the 1689 French Pickets to the 1709 Fort Schuyler and the 1757 Fort Anne.
A 2024 discovery revealed a 1901 Sanborn Fire Insurance Map of Fort Ann in the Library of Congress, offering a rare glimpse of the village’s past.
